title: manifest-eyes / Manifest
description: A manifest declares the dependency-ordered packets that compose a single piece of AI-native delivery work. Each packet is claimed exactly once, executed by a named model tier, and completed with a result reference. Stages run in order; orphan artefacts cannot be certified.

manifest_id.description: Stable slug for this manifest, used as the directory name and the claim-ledger key.

brief.description: The originating operator brief that sourced this manifest. Verbatim where possible.

depth.description: Determines how many stages run and how strict the assessor consensus requirement is. 'smoke' skips eval; 'deep' runs eval+refine+verify with hard 2-of-3 floor.

stages.description: Ordered list of packets. Position in array is dependency order; a packet may not be claimed before all earlier packets are complete.

operator.description: The human ultimately accountable for the manifest. Distinct from the model tier executing each packet.

tags.description: Free-form tags for retrieval. Convention: client-slug, brand-namespace, recognition-track, etc.

stage.description: Pipeline stage. Stages must execute in this order. 'evaluate' is the eyes-consensus stage and requires model_tier=external-diverse.

model_tier.description: Which tier of model executes this packet. 'external-diverse' means three independent assessor providers (see eyes-config.example.json).

depends_on.description: Packet IDs that must be in 'complete' status before this packet may be claimed.

expected_output_type.description: What the result reference must be. manifest-complete validates the result type matches; mismatch is rejected.

consensus_floor.description: Required consensus on assessor stages. Two dissents block under '2-of-3'; one dissent blocks under '3-of-3'; 'advisory' records dissents without blocking.

result_ref.description: Path or URI to the artefact produced by this packet. Required to mark complete.
